mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of labor market tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates strong demand outpacing supply, which forces corporate pricing power upward and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in 65% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, with a negative Rank IC below -0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumer expenditure, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5) across a 5-year out-of-sample test, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a collapsing construction sector and reduced household wealth formation, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return distribution shows a mean above 0.0% in 80% of cases where housing starts fall by more than 5%, invalidating the short-term bearish signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while consumer price inflation simultaneously declines, it confirms a successful disinflationary tightening cycle that preserves real corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising rates and falling inflation yields negative 20-day returns in 60% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for yield curve slope.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% during a mid-cycle expansion phase confirms robust economic momentum that supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 60-day returns during mid-cycle phases, with a negative cumulative return of -2.0% or lower in a walk-forward test.
